stories.headline: Vercel Breach Sends Crypto Devs Racing to Rotate API Keys Across Live Apps
stories.tldr: A security incident at hosting provider Vercel may have exposed customer API keys, forcing crypto teams to rotate credentials and audit code paths in production.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A single infra dependency just became a supply-chain risk for half the front ends in crypto — any leaked RPC, indexer, or admin key is a potential rug vector until rotated.
the_details: Vercel disclosed the breach April 20, warning that customer environment variables and API tokens could have been accessed by attackers.
Crypto projects using Vercel for front-end hosting are rotating Alchemy, Infura, Helius, WalletConnect, and treasury keys as a precaution.
Comes one day after the $292M Kelp DAO exploit — security teams now treating April as a full-stack infrastructure emergency.

stories.headline: Aave Bleeds $9B in TVL as Kelp Contagion Turns Into DeFi's Worst Bank Run of 2026
stories.tldr: Aave's total value locked collapsed from $26.4B to roughly $20B over the weekend as depositors fled following the $292M Kelp DAO exploit.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The biggest DeFi lender just proved that even fully collateralized money markets run on trust — one cross-chain bridge failure cascaded into a multi-billion-dollar withdrawal wave in 48 hours.
the_details: Aave TVL fell from $26.4B on April 18 to roughly $20B by Sunday morning as rsETH depositors pulled liquidity.
AAVE token is down 22.9% over the weekend, leading CoinDesk 20 laggards.
Whales opened nearly $24M in AAVE longs betting on a rebound, while SparkLend and Fluid also froze rsETH markets.

stories.headline: SEC Greenlights DeFi Front Ends: No Broker-Dealer Registration for Covered UI Providers
stories.tldr: The SEC's Division of Trading and Markets confirmed it will not object to software interfaces facilitating crypto asset security trades without broker-dealer registration.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This is the first U.S. regulatory green light for non-custodial DeFi front ends — a structural unlock for builders who have spent five years operating under enforcement ambiguity.
the_details: Statement issued April 13 covers 'Covered User Interface Providers' that don't hold customer assets or exercise trading discretion.
Builds on the March 11 SEC-CFTC MoU signed by Chairmen Atkins and Selig, plus March 17 joint token taxonomy.
Conditions include transparent disclosures and no custody — expected to trigger a wave of U.S.-based DEX front-end relaunches.

stories.headline: Bitmine Scoops $230M of ETH in Biggest Weekly Buy of 2026, Nears 5M ETH Treasury
stories.tldr: Ethereum treasury firm Bitmine purchased 101,627 ETH worth over $230M last week, its largest weekly accumulation of the year as price dipped under $2,300.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Corporate ETH treasuries are now buying the Kelp-driven dip at scale — Bitmine alone is closing in on 5M ETH, concentrating supply in public-company hands the way Strategy did with BTC.
the_details: 101,627 ETH acquired, worth roughly $230M at weekend prices near $2,263.
Takes Bitmine's stack close to 5 million ETH — the largest corporate ETH holding on record.
ETH bounced to $2,307 Monday morning despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire ending; institutional bid absorbing retail selling.

stories.headline: Strategy Buys 34,164 BTC for $2.54B — Third-Largest Purchase on Record
stories.tldr: Michael Saylor's Strategy added 34,164 BTC for $2.54B last week, funded via STRC perpetual preferred and common stock issuance as Bitcoin tested $75K support.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Strategy is using the geopolitical-driven weakness to keep its flywheel spinning — preferred-stock-funded BTC accumulation continues to set the institutional floor under price.
the_details: 34,164 BTC purchased for $2.54B, the third-largest single-week buy in company history.
Funded by STRC perpetual preferred stock sales plus common equity issuance — no convertible debt this round.
BTC opened Monday at $73,820 after weekend U.S.-Iran tensions, rallied to $75,242 by 7:35 a.m. ET.
